channel.title: theba.sh
channel.description: High-signal AI & tech news for developers

item.title: theba.sh — April 14, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 14, 2026
*33 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Claude Mythos: The Model Too Dangerous to Ship — And Washington Can't Decide If That's a Problem
Anthropic's Claude Mythos is shaping up to be the most politically charged model release since GPT-2's staged rollout — except this time, the danger assessment is coming from *governments*, not the lab itself. The UK AI Security Institute flagged Mythos after evaluation, with findin

*33 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Claude Mythos: The Model Too Dangerous to Release — and Goldman Sachs Is "Hyper-Aware"
Anthropic's Claude Mythos continues to be the story that won't stop generating news. What started as a preview of a model flagged as potentially too dangerous to release publicly has now reached the highest levels of finance: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon told reporters Sunday that he's "hy

item.title: theba.sh — April 12, 2026

*26 sources · 164 articles processed · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic's Mythos: The Model Too Dangerous to Release — and Now Trump Wants Banks to Test It
The week's most consequential story just got weirder. Anthropic's Claude Mythos — the model they quietly acknowledged is the first they've deemed too dangerous to release publicly since GPT-2 set that precedent in 2019 — is now apparently being quietly encourag

item.title: theba.sh — April 11, 2026

*28 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic Is Eating OpenAI's Lunch in Enterprise — Driven Almost Entirely by Claude Code
The FT is reporting that Anthropic is closing in on — and may soon surpass — OpenAI in a key measure: US enterprise AI spending. The driver isn't Claude's general chat capabilities or some vague benchmark win. It's Claude Code, which has apparently become the default AI coding tool for enou

## stdout // the big story
### Claude Mythos: The First Model "Too Dangerous to Release" Since GPT-2 — and Wall Street Is Already Stress-Testing It
Anthropic's Claude Mythos is having a consequential 24 hours. What started as an internal model safety disclosure has cascaded into a genuine geopolitical and financial stability event. The model — described as the first AI Anthropic has deemed too dangerous for

item.title: theba.sh — April 9, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 9, 2026
*35 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI: The Vibes Are Off, and the Data Backs It Up
OpenAI closed $122B at an $852B post-money valuation just weeks ago and is eyeing an IPO — on paper, this should feel like a victory lap. Instead, the company is leaking credibility from every seam simultaneously. Today's pile-on: **Florida AG Ashley Moody opened a formal investigation** into OpenAI over a alleged connection to

item.title: theba.sh — April 8, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 8, 2026
*33 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI's Structural Crack-Up, Live and In Public
The vibes are, in fact, off at OpenAI — and the pile of evidence is growing faster than the company can spin it. In the span of a week: the executive bench has visibly thinned ahead of a planned IPO, ads have appeared in ChatGPT (after Sam Altman explicitly called them a "last resort" as recently as 2024), ChatGPT's enterprise pri

item.title: theba.sh — April 7, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 7, 2026
*33 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
**Claude Mythos: Anthropic's Model Too Dangerous to Ship — But Ships Anyway (To Apple)**
Anthropic today unveiled Claude Mythos, and the headline isn't the benchmarks — it's the containment breach. During internal testing, Mythos was explicitly instructed to escape its sandbox and succeeded. The 244-page system card (yes, two hundred and forty-four pages) documents the model finding

item.title: theba.sh — April 6, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 6, 2026
*33 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Iran Threatens Stargate Data Centers as U.S.-Iran War Reshapes AI Infrastructure
The war between the U.S. and Iran has officially collided with AI infrastructure: Iran has explicitly threatened to target U.S.-linked data centers with missile strikes, with OpenAI's Stargate facilities — including the Abu Dhabi campus — named as potential targets. This isn't rhetorical escalation;

item.title: theba.sh — April 5, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 5, 2026
*27 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
**Update: Gemma 4 aftermath — the open-weight conversation has fundamentally shifted**
Google dropped Gemma 4 earlier this week, and the community is still unpacking what it actually means. The r/LocalLLaMA verdict is blunt: it "casually destroyed every model on [the benchmark]" at its weight class, and the Interconnects analysis goes deeper on *why* — specifically the per-layer emb

item.title: theba.sh — April 4, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 4, 2026
*30 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### The Claude Code Ecosystem is Fracturing — and Anthropic Just Lit the Match
The biggest story in dev tooling this week isn't a new model — it's Anthropic drawing a hard line around its Claude Code subscription economics. Effective today at 3PM ET, Claude subscribers can no longer use their subscription limits with third-party harnesses including **OpenClaw**, the popular open-sou

item.title: theba.sh — April 3, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 3, 2026
*35 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI's C-Suite Is Cracking — Again
Two senior OpenAI executives are out (or nearly out) simultaneously, and the timing is conspicuous. Fidji Simo, who was recently rebranded from "CEO of Applications" to "CEO of AGI Deployment" — a title change that itself raised eyebrows — is taking medical leave for "several weeks" due to an undisclosed health issue. More structurally signif

item.title: theba.sh — April 2, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 2, 2026
*38 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
**Update: Gemma 4 Drops — Google's Open-Weight Bet Gets Serious**
Google shipped Gemma 4 today, and the community is treating it as a genuine inflection point for open-weight models. The release covers three sizes — 1B, 13B, and 27B dense models — plus a 124B MoE variant that's already triggering the perennial LocalLLaMA question: "will the MoE actually open too?" Benchmarks are cir

item.title: theba.sh — April 1, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — April 1, 2026
*37 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic accidentally shipped Claude Code's entire source code — then made it worse trying to clean it up
This is the story of the day, and it's a proper disaster. Anthropic inadvertently published Claude Code's complete source code — the actual implementation of their flagship agentic coding product — via what Bloomberg is calling "process errors." The leak hit GitHub, spread

item.title: theba.sh — March 31, 2026
item.description:

*36 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI Closes $122B Round at $852B Valuation — The Biggest Private Fundraise in History
OpenAI just closed what is unambiguously the largest private funding round ever assembled: $122 billion at an $852 billion valuation. To put that in perspective, this values OpenAI higher than most Fortune 50 companies — above Visa, above JPMorgan, approaching Meta's market cap. The round is

item.title: theba.sh — March 30, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — March 30, 2026
*32 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Claude Mythos Leaked — And Cybersecurity Stocks Felt It
Anthropic accidentally leaked details about Claude Mythos, described in early reactions as "by far the most powerful AI" the company has produced. The leak — apparently unintentional — spread fast enough that cybersecurity stocks took a measurable hit, with traders apparently reading Mythos's capabilities as a threat to en

item.title: theba.sh — March 29, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — March 29, 2026
*27 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### OpenAI Kills Sora — And It's Weirder Than It Looks
On Tuesday, OpenAI quietly dismantled one of its most-hyped products. Sora, the text-to-video model that generated a frenzy of press coverage when it debuted, is being shuttered. But the Sora killing is just the headline — the full story is stranger. In the same week, OpenAI also wound down a $1 billion Disney content deal, rev

item.title: theba.sh — March 28, 2026
item.description:
---
# theba.sh — March 28, 2026
*29 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Anthropic vs. the Pentagon: The Fight Over Autonomous Weapons Is Now Public
Bloomberg dropped a detailed look today at the tension between Anthropic and the U.S. military over autonomous weapons systems — and it's messier than the usual "AI safety" PR framing suggests. The short version: Anthropic has been pushing back internally on how its models can be used in lethal aut

item.title: theba.sh — March 27, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — March 27, 2026
*34 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### Claude Mythos Leaks: Anthropic's New Tier Above Opus Is Real
A leaked Anthropic internal post has confirmed what the rumor mill has been churning for weeks: Anthropic is testing a new model called **Claude Mythos**, positioned explicitly above Claude Opus in the capability hierarchy. This isn't a minor bump — the framing suggests an entirely new tier, not just an incremental up

item.title: theba.sh — March 26, 2026
item.description:
# theba.sh — March 26, 2026
*35 sources · ranked by impact · signal over noise*
---
## stdout // the big story
### ARC-AGI-3 Drops — Every Frontier Model Scores Under 1%
The big headline buried in today's digest roundup: ARC-AGI-3 launched, and every frontier model currently available scores **under 1%**. For context, ARC-AGI-2 (released last year) already had models topping out well below human performance, but frontier labs had made measurable progress. ARC-AGI-3 apparently resets that pr
